text: i really could hardly believe my ears when i not only got an appointment the next day as a new patient but also received an eye exam including changes to my prescription and walked out with glasses the same day i was told my prescription was in stock and that they would be ready in 25 minutes sooooo worth the commute to the city from new jersey which i did because of the consistently amazing reviews i really cant believe i am waiting for my glasses right now every other place ive been over the years has been a long wait to even get seen and then another 7 10 days on top of that for the glasses the selection here is so trendy as well tory burch dior prada tom ford gucci and more  i had basically a personal shopper walk around with a tray for me holding the glasses as i tried them on he was so awesome and did not push me to get the most expensive ones he really seemed like he cared and tried to get to know what i was looking for to offer genuine suggestions and he actually encouraged the cheaper one jeff is the best i really cant believe this place  i have nothing but exceeded expectations leaving here they take vba insurance so for 130 bucks i walked out same day with a new eye exam prescription changes and tory burch glasses can it get any better

categories: Health & Medical, Shopping, Fashion, Optometrists, Accessories, Eyewear & Opticians

text:
casita was the best kept secret on 4th street until the building was sold out from under them and they had to move  now they join the burgeoning restaurant scene in grand central a stones throw from taco bus

i like both places but casita taqueria is distinctly different from the yellow bus in a number of ways the tacos are all handmade and therefore thicker no need for doubling up the fish tacos are not battered but taste superfresh and healthy with their signature pineapple salsa and crema  the menu overall tastes healthier with greater emphasis on fresh flavors  though the standard menu is small with the focus on the tacos theres an everchanging list of specials depending on what is fresh and in season  you can never go wrong with any of them  try the tortilla soup and the mexican salads  the cinnamondusted red sangria is also great

the staff is always sweet and friendly and do their utmost to get the food out fresh and fast even when the line is out the door  cant wait to visit them in their new location
name: Casita Taqueria
categories: Restaurants, Mexican, Food

text:
came in yesterday well after the lunch rush and it was empty  im sure the staff was happy to have that downtime  thats a great time to get caught up on side work paper work whatever you cant seem to get to during an insane lunch rush

what turned me on to this place was the fact that they had sandwiches on seeded french bread  so right off the starting block i knew one thing  no matter what i ordered it was going on seeded french bread  i settled on the cajun bacon turkey melt and a cup of the soup which ill get to later  the sandwich was dynamite  the combination of the turkey cajun bacon provel and ltm was really good  so much so that the bacon still retained some of its crisp which was a great textural counterpoint to the softer items between the bread all enveloped by the subtlety of sesame seed

so on to the soup  the soup of the day was lobster bisque  it certainly tasted of lobster but there were no pieces of lobster in it  furthermore it was runny  bisques are by their nature a heartier thicker soup  this soup was certainly rich but its consistency was sort of between halfandhalf and vitamin d milk  maybe the addition of a little flour or cornstarch to thicken it would help  a splash of cream sherry would really bring the whole dish together along with some chunks of lobster

from a service perspective everything was great  the staff will take your order make your sandwich to order and then bring it out to you  while that was perfectly fine after the lunch rush im sure it can be insanity during a lunch rush  either way the service was great

so despite my soup experience the sandwich was great  thats why im giving nsd 4 out of 5 stars  will i be back  absolutely  theyve got other soups on the menu and other amazing sandwiches to try
name: Ninth Street Deli at Howards
categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Bars, American (New), Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Delis, Nightlife, American (Traditional)

text:
i grabbed a groupon deal to bring my fiancee here while there werent any completely unforgivable things here there wasnt anything positive to note either aside from some admirable attempts at recovering the situation by the server

we arrived and were seated quickly we placed our orders me for the corn risotto her for linguini with clam sauce shell write about her meal in her review but there was an incident involving miscommunication from the kitchen regarding her dish

plating at this place is bizarre to put it kindly partially cooked and raw vegetables were stuck into the dishes in an artful fashion to the point that i thought her linguini with clam sauce was a pasta primavera at first i was honestly confused by the plating

my risotto was pointless overcooked rice mush plated with the whole pallet of the aforementioned cooked partially cooked and raw veggies some soggy mushrooms and a dollop of goats cheese in the middle with corn mixed through at the end of the meal i realized that the corn was either canned or frozen this should ultimately have been obvious from seeing it on the menu since this isnt exactly sweetcorn season which is why you have you know season menus

i know risotto is a time consuming dish and is sensitive to overcooking but many restaurants manage to parcook their rice and successfully finish it to the right level without overcooking or taking forever

i think the most damning thing is that i didnt finish my meal this is a very odd thing for me as i was raised to clean my plate unless im full unto bursting which i wasnt here 

other than our meal my fiancee had two glasses of wine supposed to be the same not sure how they screwed it up but the wines were completely different the server though was a sweetheart and offered an on the house dessert for our troubles with her meal and comped the erroneous wine we also sat and watched a salad languish in the window for a timed 15 minutes while waiting for our food

we passed on the dessert as it appeared to just be a sysco food service selection

i think there was a reason that we were the youngest patrons in the place by between 15 and 20 years
name: Luna Bella
categories: Caterers, Restaurants, Italian, Event Planning & Services

text:
more than just another fast food place

culvers serves a typical menu of burgers chicken fries shakes etc but manages to kick up the food quality a bit i really really enjoy the cheese curds which are very similar to bite size mozz sticks but so much better all in all the burgers are pretty good but also extra greasy however the healthier menu items alone dont really justify a trip to culvers in the first place so you really have to commit to being very bad before you walk through that door if you want to make the most of it they do have very very good cement mixers which are similar to mc flurries but much much better 

the chicken is ok but if your in the mood for chicken there are much better choices out there this is a burger side shake kind of place and they do them well

i wouldve rated culvers higher but the prices are a bit high and food is terrible for you i just couldnt justify eating a heavy burger cheese curds and a shake every dayhowever i do manage to get over there at least once every 6 months

went to el sol de tala last with a small group of friends including a few whove never been to the restaurant and wow everyone thoroughly enjoyed the food and service

they have recently modified the menu which included adding vegetarian items in the main menu instead of utilizing a separate menu thanks  lots of vegetarian choices make the restaurant an ideal choice but im not sure if any vegan items are available 

chips and salsa are good but would like them to have a bit more salt on them chips besides popcorn one of the only food items i actually want salted

great service good prices i only had water so that helps and excellent food desserts looked good but i resisted the temptation and just watched as my friends enjoyed great looking desserts

text:
where do i start

limoncello has always been a staple for a great italian meal i have eaten here numerous times for various different occasions always a busy place but well managed

i will start with the food i have ordered just about everything on their menu and they always seem to exceed my expectations my favorite meal by far is the chicken and sausage scallopini 

cocktails and other adult beverages from the bar are always made properly not too strong and definitely not watered down

i love going to dinner in west chester but limoncello always seems to capture the perfect atmosphere sometimes being too busy is a turnoff but in the case of limoncello it is a pleasant surprise and makes it feel as if you are apart of the town and at the place to be i always plan and make a reservation and i have not once waited more than twothree minutes to be seated with my reservation of course some restaurants you still end up waiting like everyone else but not in the case of limoncello

service is always an a for the last 45 times that i have eaten dinner at this establishment i have had the waiter marty he always provides fast and attentive service i can always tell he has a lot of tables but somehow manages to be on top of all of them this man is a workhorse and i always tip him generously and is probably the main reason that i always return

i have to say this is the 1st review i have ever made for help and i feel compelled to do so to save diners from getting ripped off the way i did my girlfriend and i stopped by here on a sunday night and were seated right away outside looked nice and was interesting being under the highwayroad above us our server greeted us and asked if this was our first time here we said yes and from that point it seemed like the scam was on my gf ordered a glass a wine and he suggested getting a bottle because they offer it half off on sunday seems like a great deal so i ordered a margherita pizzatasted good not greatand she ordered chicken meatballs it was a bit cold and the sauce was runnyand overpriced so we get the bill and see the bottle was charged for 29 dollars i ask him about this and he said the bottle costs 59 dollars i am certainly not a wine know it all so i decided to check the average price of it the bottle of wine that in riva charged us and other diners 58 was 10

text:
made a detour into garagiste meadery for a drink on saturday evening sampled four of the different styles they had on tap reboog noir 95 cab ride sofla fruit punch and hondo

quality
i have very limited experience with mead other than knowing it is made with honey and it is somewhere between beer and wine it has never really been something i have sought out i have tried a few and was left generally unimpressed well garagiste meadery has made believers out of both my girlfriend and myself after hearing about the mystical reboog at three different places in tampa we knew that was where to start this is their pbj mead and we got the reboog noir this addition made with a blackberry touch it was incredible a liquid sandwich all the right notes and no sense that you are actually consuming a drink with 14 abv i was also a big fan of hondo a meadowfoam mead with coffee sweet but not too sweet with hints of coffee and a wonderful aftertaste as for sofla fruit punch it was more in line with my expectations this mix of mango passionfruit guava guanoabana and plantains was just far too sweet for me 95 cab ride which was based on a cabernet was like a bad strong wine but did i mention the reboogs you should get it and then get it again

quantity
the sample pour i got was 3oz while a full pour was 6oz perfect to provide a taste of the offerings and then let you dive into your favorite which honestly will probably be a reboog

value
sample sizes were 7 while a full pour fell between 11 and 13 as this was my first trip to a meadery it is impossible to gauge the deal but i was happy paying these prices for their creations

ambiance
garagiste meadery is located in tampa heights a quickly developing area in tampa in an area full of hip breweries restaurants and coffee shops it fits perfectly into the scene the meadery has a small outdoor area which mades up part of this creepy thoroughfare between them and a nearby brewery inside the space is very open and bright honestly a little too bright with those fluorescent lights you typically find in hospitals they have a bar on one side equipment along the back wall and then a bunch of tables lining the rest of the space they have two televisions one over the bar and one in the back corner and a shelf full of games for guests hanging out a while hiphop music played in the background but it seemed like they were going to shift audio over to the television as we left

crowd
it was us and one other couple who also wandered over from hidden springs mead hasnt caught on in the same way as craft beer but i would not be surprised if that changed quickly with the work they are doing here and with the success of their reboog series i expect this place to be all the rage

service
got to spend some time chatting with the owner and hear about how the place came about he was extremely friendly and happy to talk to us about mead the area and other great spots in tampa as one of the newer kids on the block he is doing a great job at getting their name out

parking
coming from hidden springs ale works we just walked through the alleyway that separates the two businesses they dont have a designated parking lot but street parking is available in the area

extra
garagiste meadery is pretty awesome while i am still not completely sold on mead as a regular beverage i now know they can make it pretty darn good i will be dreaming about reboog until i can have it again and that makes this a place anyone mead lover or not will enjoy
name: Garagiste Meadery
categories: Food, Breweries, Arts & Entertainment, Wineries, Meaderies

what is better than a burger joint that is open til 3am so i went there the other night and the service was a littler on the slower side i would say 1520 min wait the worst part about it is that its next to rumor and a lot of drunk people wander in screaming on top of that it gets really crowded and space becomes really tight 

lets get to the good stuff so i ordered the classic combo and this is what i thought
the quality of the meat was pretty good not as great as say butcher and singer or pyt but still pretty decent the temperature was pretty fair i would not say spot on but i would have to cut them some slack since it was at 145 am and they were swamped ideally i would need to try it again at a different time when they are not as crowded and on their a game the fries i thought were pretty good but i wasnt a fan of how they add extra oil even though it was so good after they had been cooked if you stand at the counter youll see what i mean without a doubt ill definitely be back there

text:
oh my god i cant believe i didnt review this place already dont tell william the owner hed kill me

this is the place you go for good coffee thats cheaper than most other places this is the place you go because its located very conveniently in the heart of broad ripple bonusit has free wifi 

really though this is the place you keep coming back to because of its warm inviting community everyone becomes a regular here

the baristas all have their own personalities dan the man fahrner is part of popular local band everthus the deadbeats tatjana byrd is a formidable local poet carly cooke is superfriendly at first but longer conversation reveals her hilarious humor this is like the saturday night live of local coffee places who needs indy fringe 

oh yeah the drinks are pretty good too the white chocolate mochas are the best ive ever tasted anywhere not too cloying not terribly sweet smooth the oatmeal shagbark cookies are absolutely addictive and the smoothies and juices are made onthefly from your selection of fresh fruit thank god they got their juicer fixedfor a while it sounded like they were blending bricks inside joke

im torn between meh and aok i stopped by butters soul food because i was craving fried chicken i got a fried chicken breast i really wanted thigh but they were out with mac  cheese and greens the chicken smelled amazing but wasnt really anything to write home about it wasnt bad it just didnt fulfill my particular craving for a particular type of fried chicken having worked around the corner from hill country chicken in nyc all summer kind of spoiled me for fried chicken 
the mac and cheese was comforting not gourmet in any way but what youd get at your grandmas house in the country the greens were the same way tasty comforting

text:
i had a sudden crave for sushi last night and passed by this brand new restaurant last night my wife and daughter wanted to give them a try and glad we did

my daughter ordered a california roll from kids menu and it came out with a very cute 2 components bento box which had cali roll and fries my daughter chose an organic milk with her meal and total was only 6 

my wife and i ordered a bento box hot and sour soup was delivered to our table right after we sat down soup was very tasteful chicken teriyaki in the box was perfectly cooked it was tender and there was a baby bokchoy vegetable underneath the meat and it was yummy with the sauce there were two pieces of vege spring rolls came with the box extremely fresh vegetables inside was crunchy and had lots of flavors the portion of the bento box was so big that my wife ahd to ask me to share with her 

i ordered couple of special rolls because i noticed the price is fairly cheap like only 11 for most of sushi restaurants probably will charge 1314 pop this roll is a cooked roll i also ordered ocean waves which has raw tuna and salmon on top i got my rolls less than 10 minutes with the bento box we were kinda surprised by how fast they make sushi the presentation of the roll was great rice melted in my mouth and they were full of flavors with the sauces the raw fish was extremely fresh i have to say they are definitely one of the best sushi i ever had 

we couldnt finish the food we ordered cause the portion was too big my wife packed the rest to take them home overall  its a cool place with very relaxing atmosphere price is cheap but doesnt compromise the quality food comes out really quick which is perfect for people who want good sushi but hate to wait i absolutely recommend this new restaurant and definitely will go back soon
name: Sushipop
categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Japanese

text:
i have been to more thai restaurants than i can count and have never found a bad one this one is special i went for lunch and was pleasantly surprised from the moment i walked in the place is comfortable and the tables are spaced far enough apart so that i dont have to listen to someone elses conversation i split an order of tom yum soup with my lovely lunch date it is a thai version of hot and sour soup and was a unique combination of subtle flavors that made each mouthful an event the entree was padthai and was every bit as good as the king and i the one complaint i had was that it could have been hotter but since i was given the choice of hotness i only have my self to blame 
i dont normally consider service that big of a part of my restaurant experience unless it is so bad that it was insulting but this was different i felt that the owner and the waiter considered us guests in their home 
all in all this was a most enjoyable experience i will make the half hour trip to visit this place so evening to savor their dinner menu
name: Thai House
categories: Thai, Restaurants, Noodles, Vegetarian

text:

several long time friends of mine from the broad ripple area all told me to try this new place out

4 i ordered the grilled chicken sandwich  it was unevenly cooked  one end was dry and chewy  the other end i had to double check to make sure it wasnt raw  it had that odd crunchy feel of undercooked chicken

5 the house fries are a sliced potato that is deep fried  just meh  some way overcooked and some chewy

6 please get rid of the generic ketchupcondiments  at a minimum go with red gold  a local company  again in the ibj article you talk about how everything is freshly made and locally sourced

7 loved our waitress



they say they are open for breakfast on the weekends  ok does 10 am really mean breakfast  their ibj article says they are going after an older crowd  to be honest an older crowd is already hours into their day at 10 am   our breakfast group meets at 830 because most of us are small business owners and have things to get done



thankfully the chumley stench has been irradicated

text:
well it wasnt the worst experience ive ever had but i will say it could have gone smoother

my mother and i have been verizon customers for a long time and im happy to say i do not have to visit their stores frequently because we have few problems with our devices and service i stopped in recently because it was time to upgrade my device

right off the bat i was told i couldnt upgrade because of a billing issue on the account my rep told me we were still receiving one lump bill for our wireless service and home service packages land line internet etc he said this had been discontinued and we should have automatically started receiving separate bills but for whatever reason it hadnt happened on our account he said she had to give consent to unsubscribe from onebill and that he needed to speak with her directly

i havent lived with my mom in a long time so i took him at his word but i wasnt able to get a hold of her so i was turned away  very irritating when you consider the only reason i can see to block an upgrade is to force action on the issue and that we apparently needed to consent for something that should have automatically happened im sure there are reasons that im just not aware of for requiring consent but what annoyed me was that neither myself or my mothers husband were allowed to give that consent i had to leave the store empty handed and come back another time 

long story short after contacting my mother and working on the problem i found out we had been unsubscribed from one bill for a while

my rep apologized for the problem which doesnt replace my wasted time but was still nice of him to do from there we proceeded with the upgrade and i bombarded him with questions about a variety of things he answered all of them and was very knowledgeable as i have known verizon reps to be im very happy with the service i received after clearing up the one bill mixup 

im now the proud owner of a droid turbo 2 and hopefully there are no future issues with upgrading i wish i could give three and a half stars because i think four is too generous for the amount of wasted time and phone tag i had to endure but i also think three is too low considering the long history of good service ive had

edit i came back to mention that never was i told i could trade in my old device for any sort of store credit it turns out my device was worth about 26 which would have been nice to know when i spent 50 on a new case but hey sales i guess
name: Verizon
categories: Internet Service Providers, Mobile Phones, Electronics, Professional Services, Shopping, Home Services
